posts.author: EnoughGunControl
posts.title: 121 years before Karmelo Anthony stabbed Austin Metcalf, there was a similar case in Kansas City. The differences were that a gun was involved, arrest to sentence only lasted 2 months, and the suspect was hanged.

twitter.content: On April 12, 1904, some white high-school boys were having track practice at a public park. According to witnesses, a group of 15 or 20 black boys were taunting and jeering them from the stands. Roy Martin, the team captain, asked them to stop. One of them shot him in the heart.

parentTweet.content: This author states that Kansas City established separate schools after "clashes between the races . . . finally culminated in a tragedy" where "a white boy was the victim." He doesn't say anything else about what happened, so I looked it up. The incident was a shooting in 1905.
